response: {"headword":"aboard","explanation":"此字兼具介系詞和副詞兩種詞性，通常指「在（船、飛機、火車等）上」或「登上（船、飛機等）」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"aboard","explanation":"作為副詞，表示「在（交通工具）上」或「往（交通工具）上」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"All passengers are requested to be <aboard> the train at least ten minutes before its scheduled departure time this evening.","translation":"所有乘客請在今晚火車預定出發時間前至少十分鐘登車。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"go aboard","explanation":"「登上（船、飛機等）」的常用片語。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The tour guide instructed everyone to <go> <aboard> the cruise ship immediately after completing the security check at the port.","translation":"導遊指示大家在港口完成安檢後立即登上遊輪。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"on board","explanation":"「在（船、飛機等）上」的常用片語。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After a brief safety demonstration, all passengers settled into their seats while the plane was <on> the runway, ready for takeoff.","translation":"簡短的安全示範後，所有乘客在飛機於跑道上準備起飛時安頓好座位。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"acceptable","explanation":"形容詞，意思是「可接受的；合意的」。常考其搭配介系詞 of 或 for，以及與 un- 構成反義字 acceptable。注意與 accept (v.) 的區別。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"acceptable","explanation":"「可接受的；令人滿意的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The committee considered the proposal to be <acceptable>, provided that a few minor adjustments were made to the budget allocation.","translation":"該委員會認為這項提議是可以接受的，前提是對預算分配做一些小的調整。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"make acceptable","explanation":"「使...變得可接受」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The presenter worked hard to <make> his research findings more <acceptable> to the general audience by simplifying the complex terminology.","translation":"演講者努力簡化複雜的術語，以使其研究發現對一般聽眾來說更容易被接受。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"unacceptable","explanation":"「不可接受的；無法容忍的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The school administration deemed the student's behavior as completely <unacceptable> and decided to implement disciplinary actions accordingly.","translation":"校方認為該學生的行為完全不可接受，並決定採取相應的紀律處分。"}]}],"related_forms":["accept (v.)","acceptance (n.)","acceptably (adv.)"]}

{"headword":"accurate","explanation":"形容詞，意思為「準確的；精確的」。常用於描述測量、數據、信息、猜測等。注意與 precise (精確的) 的細微差別，accurate 強調結果與事實相符，precise 強調細節的嚴謹。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"accurate","explanation":"「準確的；精確的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The weather forecast predicted heavy rain for the weekend, and fortunately, the prediction proved to be highly <accurate> based on the actual conditions.","translation":"天氣預報預測週末將有大雨，而幸運的是，根據實際情況，這個預測被證明非常準確。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"accurate information","explanation":"「準確的資訊」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"It is crucial for students to rely on <accurate> <information> from credible sources when conducting research for their academic papers.","translation":"學生在撰寫學術論文進行研究時，依賴準確的資訊來源至關重要。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"make accurate measurements","explanation":"「做出準確的測量」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The engineers used advanced equipment to <make> <accurate> <measurements> of the bridge's structural integrity before the renovation began.","translation":"在翻修開始前，工程師們使用先進的設備對橋樑的結構完整性進行了精確測量。"}]}],"related_forms":["accurately (adv.)","accuracy (n.)"]}

{"headword":"ache","explanation":"此字可作名詞（疼痛）或動詞（疼痛）。常考與身體部位的搭配，如 headache, stomachache，或與介系詞 from 搭配。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"ache","explanation":"作為名詞，指「疼痛」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After a long day of hiking in the mountains, he felt a dull <ache> in his muscles, signaling that he needed rest and recovery.","translation":"在山上健行了一整天後，他感到肌肉痠痛，這表明他需要休息和恢復。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"ache","explanation":"作為動詞，指「（身體部位）疼痛」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"She continued to <ache> from the flu for several days, making it difficult for her to concentrate on her schoolwork and daily activities.","translation":"她流感後持續痠痛了好幾天，使她難以專注於學業和日常活動。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"headache","explanation":"「頭痛」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The student mentioned that a persistent <headache> was affecting his ability to study effectively for the upcoming final exams.","translation":"該學生提到持續的頭痛影響了他有效準備即將到來的期末考的能力。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"backache","explanation":"「背痛」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Prolonged sitting at a desk without proper posture can often lead to a severe <backache> among office workers and students alike.","translation":"長時間坐在桌前卻沒有正確姿勢，經常導致上班族和學生都嚴重背痛。"}]}],"related_forms":["aches (pl.)","ached (past)","aching (pres. part.)"]}

{"headword":"achieve","explanation":"動詞，意為「達成；實現」。常與名詞 achievement (成就) 搭配考，或是與介系詞 of 搭配表示「達成...的目標」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"achieve","explanation":"「達成；實現」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Through dedication and consistent effort, the team managed to <achieve> their ambitious goal of winning the national championship title this year.","translation":"透過奉獻和持續的努力，該團隊設法實現了他們今年贏得全國冠軍頭銜的雄心勃勃的目標。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"achieve success","explanation":"「獲得成功」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many successful entrepreneurs attribute their ability to <achieve> <success> to a combination of hard work, strategic planning, and resilience.","translation":"許多成功的企業家將他們獲得成功的能力歸因於勤奮、策略規劃和韌性的結合。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"achieve a balance","explanation":"「達到平衡」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"It is often challenging for students to <achieve> a healthy <balance> between their academic responsibilities and extracurricular activities.","translation":"學生們要在學業責任和課外活動之間達到健康的平衡，這常常是充滿挑戰的。"}]}],"related_forms":["achievement (n.)","achieved (past)","achieving (pres. part.)"]}

{"headword":"additional","explanation":"此為形容詞，意指「額外的、附加的」，常用於描述某物是原有的之外的補充。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"additional","explanation":"作為形容詞，表示「額外的」或「附加的」。注意與副詞 additionally (此外) 的區別。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Students may be required to purchase <additional> textbooks and materials for specialized courses in higher education.","translation":"大學的專業課程可能會要求學生購買額外的教科書與教材。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"in addition to","explanation":"此片語為「除了...之外」，表示包含或不包含某事物，但通常表示包含。與 besides 意思相近。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"<In addition to> the required courses, students are encouraged to take elective subjects that align with their career aspirations.","translation":"除了必修課程之外，也鼓勵學生選修與其職涯抱負一致的選修科目。"}]}],"related_forms":["additionally (adv.)","addition (n.)"]}

{"headword":"admire","explanation":"此為動詞，意指「欽佩、欣賞」，通常用於讚賞某人的成就、特質或美麗的事物。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"admire","explanation":"表示「欽佩」或「欣賞」。常與 for 搭配，說明欽佩的原因。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many aspiring young athletes <admire> the dedication and perseverance shown by Olympic medalists in achieving their dreams.","translation":"許多有抱負的年輕運動員欽佩奧運獎牌得主為實現夢想所展現的奉獻和毅力。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"admire someone for something","explanation":"「因為某事而欽佩某人」。是常見的搭配用法，值得記憶。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The professor is widely <admired> *for* her groundbreaking research in artificial intelligence and her inspirational teaching methods.","translation":"這位教授因其在人工智能領域的開創性研究和鼓舞人心的教學方法而廣受讚譽。"}]}],"related_forms":["admiration (n.)","admiring (adj.)"]}

{"headword":"advanced","explanation":"此為形容詞，意指「先進的、高級的」，常用於描述科技、學術或技能的水平。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"advanced","explanation":"作為形容詞，表示「先進的、高級的」。可用於描述科技、學術程度或課程等級。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The university offers <advanced> placement courses in mathematics and physics for exceptionally talented high school students.","translation":"該大學為資優高中生提供數學和物理的進階學分課程。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"advanced technology","explanation":"「先進科技」。是常見的複合名詞用法，在科技相關的文章中頻繁出現。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Implementing <advanced> technology in manufacturing processes can significantly boost productivity and reduce operational costs.","translation":"在製造過程中實施先進科技可以顯著提高生產力並降低營運成本。"}]}],"related_forms":["advance (v./n.)"]}

{"headword":"advantage","explanation":"此為名詞，意指「優勢、利益」，指有利的條件或情況，使人在競爭或某方面處於有利地位。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"advantage","explanation":"指「優勢」或「利益」。常與介詞 of 搭配，表示擁有某種優勢。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The company plans to leverage its <advantage> in market research to develop innovative products that meet customer demands.","translation":"該公司計劃利用其在市場研究方面的優勢，開發滿足客戶需求的創新產品。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"take advantage of","explanation":"此片語意為「利用、佔...的便宜」。根據語境，可表示正面的「善用」或負面的「剝削」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Students should actively <take advantage of> the resources available at the university library for their academic research and personal growth.","translation":"學生應積極利用大學圖書館提供的資源來進行學術研究和個人成長。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"have an advantage over","explanation":"「比...有優勢」。表示在比較之下，某一方處於更有利的地位。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Early registration for the popular workshop gives participants an <advantage> *over* others who might miss out on limited spots.","translation":"熱門工作坊的早期報名讓參與者比其他可能錯過有限名額的人更有優勢。"}]}],"related_forms":["advantageous (adj.)"]}

{"headword":"adventure","explanation":"此為名詞，意指「冒險、奇遇」，指充滿刺激、風險或新奇經驗的活動或經歷。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"adventure","explanation":"表示「冒險」或「奇遇」。常指有風險或刺激的經歷。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Embarking on a solo backpacking trip across Southeast Asia was the most thrilling <adventure> of her college life.","translation":"獨自背包橫跨東南亞的旅行是她大學生活中最刺激的冒險。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"seek adventure","explanation":"「尋求冒險」。表示主動尋找刺激或新奇的體驗。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many young explorers feel compelled to <seek> new <adventures> and discover uncharted territories around the globe.","translation":"許多年輕的探險家覺得有必要去尋求新的冒險，並發現全球未知的領域。"}]}],"related_forms":["adventurous (adj.)"]}

{"headword":"advertise","explanation":"這個字主要與「廣告」相關，注意其動詞和名詞形式的不同。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"advertise","explanation":"動詞，指「為...做廣告」或「宣傳」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The company decided to <advertise> its new eco-friendly products through various online platforms to reach a wider audience.","translation":"該公司決定透過各種線上平台為其新的環保產品做廣告，以接觸更廣泛的受眾。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"advertisement","explanation":"名詞，指「廣告」本身。常縮寫為 advert.","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"A catchy <advertisement> on television can significantly increase public awareness of a new brand or product.","translation":"電視上引人注目的廣告可以顯著提高公眾對新品牌或產品的認識。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"adv.","explanation":"advertisement 的縮寫，意為「廣告」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many students search for part-time job opportunities through the 'Jobs' <adv.> section of the campus newspaper.","translation":"許多學生透過校報的「工作」廣告欄尋找兼職工作機會。"}]}],"related_forms":["advertisement","adv.","advertising"]}

{"headword":"advise","explanation":"此字為動詞，意為「建議」或「勸告」。其名詞形式 advicer/advisor 務必區分。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"advise","explanation":"動詞，意為「建議」或「告知」。常用句型 advise sb. to do sth. 或 advise sb. on sth.","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The experienced doctor <advise>d the patient to adopt a healthier lifestyle to manage the chronic condition effectively.","translation":"經驗豐富的醫生建議病人採取更健康的生活方式來有效管理慢性病。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"advise on","explanation":"「就...提供建議」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Financial experts are often hired to <advise> clients <on> investment strategies for long-term wealth growth.","translation":"理財專家通常被聘請就投資策略向客戶提供建議，以實現長期財富增長。"}]}],"related_forms":["advice (n.)","adviser","advisor"]}

{"headword":"adviser","explanation":"指「顧問」或「指導者」，常指在學術、職業或財務方面提供建議的人。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"adviser","explanation":"名詞，提供建議或指導的人。advisor 為同義詞，拼法不同。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Students are encouraged to consult their academic <adviser> regularly to ensure they are on track for graduation requirements.","translation":"鼓勵學生定期諮詢他們的學術指導教授，以確保他們符合畢業要求。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"advisor","explanation":"名詞，與 adviser 同義，意為「顧問」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The career <advisor> provided valuable insights and resources to help graduates navigate the challenging job market.","translation":"職涯顧問提供了寶貴的見解和資源，幫助應屆畢業生駕馭充滿挑戰的就業市場。"}]}],"related_forms":["advise (v.)","advice (n.)"]}

{"headword":"afford","explanation":"此字為動詞，主要指「負擔得起」某物的費用，或「提供」某物。注意其與不定詞的搭配。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"afford","explanation":"動詞，意為「負擔得起（金錢上）」或「提供（機會、時間等）」。常用句型 afford to do sth.","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Given the current economic situation, many young families find it difficult to <afford> to buy a house in the city center.","translation":"鑑於當前的經濟狀況，許多年輕家庭發現很難負擔得起市中心房子的購買費用。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"afford an opportunity","explanation":"「提供機會」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The internship program <afford>s students a valuable <opportunity> to gain practical experience in their chosen field.","translation":"這個實習計畫為學生提供了寶貴的機會，讓他們在所選領域獲得實務經驗。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"afterward","explanation":"此字為副詞，意為「之後」或「後來」，與 afterwards 同義。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"afterward","explanation":"副詞，指「之後」、「後來」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The lecture concluded at 5 PM, and the participants discussed the topics <afterward> over coffee in the lounge.","translation":"講座於下午五點結束，參與者隨後在休息室喝咖啡時討論了相關話題。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"afterwards","explanation":"副詞，與 afterward 同義，意為「之後」、「後來」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"We attended the school fair in the morning and went to the cinema <afterwards> with our friends.","translation":"我們早上參加了學校的園遊會，之後和朋友們一起去看電影。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"agriculture","explanation":"農業相關的單字，常與環境、科技或經濟發展結合出現。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"agriculture","explanation":"指「農業」，是關於耕種、飼養動物的科學與藝術。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Modern <agriculture> relies heavily on technology and scientific methods to improve crop yields and livestock production efficiency.","translation":"現代農業嚴重依賴科技和科學方法來提高作物產量和畜牧生產效率。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"agricultural","explanation":"形容詞，指「農業的」，用來修飾與農業相關的事物。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The government is investing in sustainable <agricultural> practices to protect the environment and ensure food security for the future.","translation":"政府正投資於可持續的農業實踐，以保護環境並確保未來的糧食安全。"}]}],"related_forms":["agricultural"]}

{"headword":"airline","explanation":"「航空公司」的常用字，注意與 aircraft (飛機) 的區別。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"airline","explanation":"指「航空公司」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The passenger was worried about missing his connecting flight due to the unexpected delay of the first <airline> service.","translation":"由於第一家航空公司的航班意外延誤，這位乘客擔心會錯過他的轉機航班。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"major airline","explanation":"「主要的航空公司」或「大航空公司」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"He dreams of working as a pilot for a <major> <airline> after graduating from the flight academy.","translation":"他夢想著從飛行學院畢業後，能為一家大航空公司擔任飛行員。"}]}],"related_forms":["airliner"]}

{"headword":"alley","explanation":"「小巷」或「後巷」，常與 street (街道) 比較。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"alley","explanation":"指狹窄的「小巷」，通常是兩棟建築物之間的小路。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The children enjoyed playing hide-and-seek in the narrow <alley> behind their houses after school every day.","translation":"放學後，孩子們每天都喜歡在他們房子後面的狹窄小巷裡玩捉迷藏。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"back alley","explanation":"「後巷」，通常指較少人經過、有時可能不太安全的通道。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The stray cat often slept peacefully in the quiet <back> <alley>, away from the hustle and bustle of the main street.","translation":"這隻流浪貓經常在安靜的後巷裡平靜地睡覺，遠離主要街道的喧囂。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"almond","explanation":"「杏仁」，一種堅果，常與 nut (堅果) 或 flavor (口味) 搭配。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"almond","explanation":"指「杏仁」這種堅果。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"She enjoys adding a handful of roasted <almond> nuts to her morning yogurt for extra protein and a satisfying crunch.","translation":"她喜歡在早晨的優格中加入一把烤杏仁，以增加蛋白質和令人滿意的脆口感。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"almond milk","explanation":"「杏仁奶」，一種植物性奶，適合素食者或對乳製品過敏者。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many people are choosing <almond> <milk> as a dairy-free alternative for their coffee and cereal due to its light taste.","translation":"由於其清淡的口味，許多人選擇杏仁奶作為咖啡和穀物的無乳製品替代品。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"alphabet","explanation":"「字母表」或「字母順序」，是學習語言的基礎。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"alphabet","explanation":"指「字母表」，例如英文字母 A to Z。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Learning the basic <alphabet> is the first crucial step for young children beginning their journey into literacy and reading.","translation":"學習基本的字母表是年輕孩子們開始識字和閱讀之旅的第一步關鍵。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"alphabetical order","explanation":"「字母順序」，常用於整理名單、詞典等。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The librarian asked the students to arrange the returned books in <alphabetical> <order> by author's last name on the shelves.","translation":"圖書館員要求學生們按照作者姓氏的字母順序將歸還的書籍整理好放在書架上。"}]}],"related_forms":["alphabetic","alphabetical"]}

{"headword":"amaze","explanation":"此字主要考驗「使...驚奇、驚艷」的動詞用法，相關名詞 amazement 和形容詞 amazing 亦常出現。注意其被動語態的考法。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"amaze","explanation":"使...驚奇、驚艷。強調令人出乎意料的程度。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The young prodigy's ability to play complex piano pieces by ear <amazed> the audience and the judges at the international competition.","translation":"這位年輕的天才僅憑聽力就能彈奏複雜的鋼琴曲，令國際比賽的觀眾和評審驚嘆不已。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"amazement","explanation":"名詞，指「驚奇、驚艷」的狀態。常與介詞 at 或 by 連用。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"To our great <amazement>, the magician made the entire car disappear from the stage in just a few seconds.","translation":"令我們非常驚訝的是，魔術師在短短幾秒鐘內就讓整輛汽車從舞台上消失了。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"amazing","explanation":"形容詞，指「令人驚奇的、極好的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The panoramic view from the mountaintop was truly <amazing>, offering a breathtaking spectacle of nature's grandeur.","translation":"從山頂上看到的壯麗全景確實令人驚嘆，提供了大自然宏偉壯麗的景象。"}]}],"related_forms":["amazed","amazing","amazement"]}

{"headword":"ambassador","explanation":"此字指「大使」，是國際關係和外交場合的重要詞彙。注意其與國家、職責的搭配。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"ambassador","explanation":"大使，指國家派遣到外國擔任最高外交代表的人。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The newly appointed <ambassador> presented his credentials to the host country's president during a formal ceremony at the palace.","translation":"新任命的大使在宮殿舉行的正式儀式上，向東道國總統遞交了他的國書。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"ambassador-at-large","explanation":"巡迴大使，通常負責特定議題或區域的外交任務，不常駐於某國。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"She serves as an <ambassador-at-large> for environmental protection, traveling globally to promote sustainable practices.","translation":"她擔任環境保護的巡迴大使，在全球各地推廣可持續發展的做法。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"ambition","explanation":"這個字表示「野心」或「抱負」，常與名詞或動詞搭配，指追求成功或成就的強烈願望。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"ambition","explanation":"名詞，指強烈的願望或抱負，尤指獲得權力、財富或成功的渴望。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"His relentless <ambition> to become a renowned scientist drove him to pursue advanced studies abroad.","translation":"他成為著名科學家的不懈抱負，驅使他出國深造。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"ambitious","explanation":"形容詞，指有雄心壯志的、野心勃勃的。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The <ambitious> project aims to revolutionize renewable energy production within the next decade.","translation":"這個雄心勃勃的項目旨在未來十年內徹底改變可再生能源的生產。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"pursue one's ambition","explanation":"追求某人的抱負/野心。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"She decided to <pursue> her lifelong <ambition> of becoming a professional artist despite financial challenges.","translation":"儘管面臨經濟上的挑戰，她仍決定追求她畢生的藝術家抱負。"}]}],"related_forms":["ambitious","ambitiously"]}

{"headword":"ambulance","explanation":"此字指「救護車」，是與緊急醫療服務相關的重要詞彙。注意其與急救、送醫的關聯。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"ambulance","explanation":"救護車，專門用來運送病患或傷患的車輛。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"When the accident happened, bystanders immediately called for an <ambulance> to transport the injured person to the hospital.","translation":"事故發生時，旁觀者立即叫了救護車將傷者送往醫院。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"call for an ambulance","explanation":"叫救護車。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"If you witness a serious injury, the first thing you should do is <call> the emergency services and ask <for> an <ambulance>.","translation":"如果你目睹了嚴重的傷害，你應該做的第一件事是撥打緊急電話並叫一輛救護車。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"angel","explanation":"此字有「天使」和「像天使一樣善良的人」兩種主要用法。注意其引申義的考法，常用於形容人。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"angel","explanation":"天使，傳說中上帝的使者；或指非常善良、美麗、有幫助的人。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The old woman described her volunteer nurse as a true <angel> for her kindness and dedication during her recovery.","translation":"這位老婦人形容她的志工護士是一位真正的天使，因為她在她康復期間所展現的善良和奉獻。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"heavenly angel","explanation":"天上的天使，強調其神聖或超凡的特質。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The choir sang a beautiful hymn, evoking the image of <heavenly> <angels> descending from above.","translation":"合唱團唱了一首優美的聖歌，讓人聯想到天上天使降臨的景象。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"guardian angel","explanation":"守護天使，被認為在暗中保護某人的人。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many people believe that everyone has a <guardian> <angel> watching over them, offering guidance and protection.","translation":"許多人相信每個人都有一個守護天使在他們身邊看顧，給予指引和保護。"}]}],"related_forms":["angelic"]}

{"headword":"announce","explanation":"此字主要考「宣布」、「預告」的用法，名詞 announcement 則指「公告」或「宣布」。注意其後常接 that 子句或 to-infinitive。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"announce","explanation":"動詞，指「公開宣布」消息、計畫等。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The school principal will <announce> the list of scholarship recipients during the morning assembly tomorrow.","translation":"校長將在明天的朝會時宣布獎學金得主名單。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"announcement","explanation":"名詞，指「公告」、「通知」或「宣布」。常搭配 make an announcement。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Please pay attention to the important <announcement> regarding the upcoming career fair for all graduating students.","translation":"請注意關於所有應屆畢業生即將到來之求職博覽會的重要公告。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"announce that","explanation":"「宣布某事」；that 後面接一個完整的子句。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The company decided to <announce> that they would be expanding their operations into the Asian market next year.","translation":"公司決定宣布他們明年將擴展業務到亞洲市場。"}]}],"related_forms":["announcement (n.)"]}

{"headword":"anxious","explanation":"此字主要考「擔憂的」、「焦慮的」之意，其後常接 about/for sth. 或 that 子句。另外，也可指「渴望」某事。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"anxious about","explanation":"「對...感到焦慮」；about 後面接擔心的事情。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many students feel <anxious> <about> their upcoming final exams, worrying about the results and their grades.","translation":"許多學生對即將到來的期末考感到焦慮，擔心考試結果和分數。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"anxious for","explanation":"「渴望」得到某物或發生某事。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After months of hard work, the team was <anxious> <for> the opportunity to showcase their innovative project.","translation":"經過數月的辛勤工作，團隊渴望有機會展示他們創新的專案。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"anxious that","explanation":"「擔心」；that 後面接表示擔心的情況。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Parents are often <anxious> <that> their children might not be able to adapt well to a new school environment.","translation":"父母經常擔心他們的孩子可能無法很好地適應新的學校環境。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"anyhow","explanation":"此字為副詞，有「無論如何」、「不管怎樣」或「以隨便的方式」之意。在 GSAT 中，常指「總之」、「不管怎麼樣」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"anyhow","explanation":"副詞，表示「無論如何」、「不管怎樣」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The weather forecast predicted heavy rain, but the hikers decided to proceed with their mountain climbing <anyhow>.","translation":"天氣預報預測有大雨，但登山客無論如何還是決定繼續他們的登山計畫。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"anyhow","explanation":"副詞，表示「以隨便、不仔細的方式」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"He completed the assignment <anyhow>, not paying much attention to the details or the required format.","translation":"他隨便地完成了作業，沒有怎麼注意細節或要求的格式。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"apart","explanation":"此字為副詞，主要有「分開」、「與眾不同」或「（時間、空間上）距離」的意思。常與 from 搭配，表示「除了...之外」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"apart","explanation":"副詞，表示「分開」或「相距」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After the graduation ceremony, the close friends decided to go <apart> and pursue their individual career goals.","translation":"畢業典禮後，親密的朋友們決定分開，追求各自的職業目標。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"apart from","explanation":"介詞片語，意為「除了...之外」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"<Apart> <from> his academic achievements, he is also known for his outstanding leadership skills in student organizations.","translation":"除了學術成就之外，他也以其在學生組織中卓越的領導能力而聞名。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"fall apart","explanation":"動詞片語，意為「散開」、「瓦解」或「崩潰」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The old wooden chair began to <fall> <apart> when the heavy student sat on it during the library study session.","translation":"那個舊木椅在圖書館自習時，當一位體重較重的學生坐上去時，開始散架了。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"apologize","explanation":"此字為動詞，指「道歉」。其後常接 for sth. 或 to sb. for sth.，名詞 apologize 為 apology。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"apologize for","explanation":"「為...道歉」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The student had to <apologize> <for> his late submission of the research paper, explaining the unforeseen circumstances.","translation":"這位學生必須為遲交研究報告道歉，並解釋了未預見的情況。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"apologize to ... for","explanation":"「向...為...道歉」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The host of the TV show decided to <apologize> <to> the audience <for> the technical difficulties that disrupted the live broadcast.","translation":"該電視節目主持人決定向觀眾道歉，因為技術故障擾亂了直播。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"apology","explanation":"名詞，指「道歉」或「歉意」。常搭配 offer an apology。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The company issued a formal <apology> on its website for the product defect that caused inconvenience to consumers.","translation":"該公司在其網站上正式發布聲明，為導致消費者不便的產品缺陷道歉。"}]}],"related_forms":["apology (n.)"]}

{"headword":"appeal","explanation":"此字彙的常見考點包含「呼籲、懇求」、「吸引力」以及「上訴」的用法。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"appeal","explanation":"作為名詞，指「訴請、懇求」或「吸引力」。常考於「吸引力」的意義，尤其是在描述某事物對某人的吸引程度。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The candidate's message of hope and change had a strong <appeal> to young voters across the nation.","translation":"這位候選人傳遞的希望與改變的訊息，對全國的年輕選民產生了強大的吸引力。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"appeal","explanation":"作為動詞，指「懇求、呼籲」或「（向法院）上訴」。常與介詞to搭配使用，表示向某人發出請求。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The environmental group will <appeal> to the public to reduce plastic waste and protect marine ecosystems.","translation":"環保組織將呼籲公眾減少塑膠廢棄物，以保護海洋生態系統。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"appeal to","explanation":"動詞片語，表示「對...有吸引力」或「懇求、訴請」。是GSAT常考的固定搭配。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The professor's research on renewable energy sources has great <appeal> to students interested in environmental science.","translation":"這位教授關於可再生能源的研究，對對環境科學感興趣的學生具有很大的吸引力。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"appeal against","explanation":"動詞片語，意為「就...提出上訴」。通常用於法律或正式的爭議場合。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The defendant decided to <appeal> *against* the court's decision, hoping for a retrial of the case.","translation":"被告決定對法院的判決提出上訴，希望能重新審理此案。"}]}],"related_forms":["appeals (v. pl.)","appealed (v. past)","appealing (v. pres. p.)"]}

{"headword":"approve","explanation":"此字彙主要考「批准、贊成」的用法，常與介詞 of 或 of...for 搭配。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"approve","explanation":"作為動詞，表示「批准、認可」或「贊同」。在學校和職場情境中都非常常用。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The school board will <approve> the new curriculum proposal after reviewing its potential impact on student learning.","translation":"校務委員會在審查新課程提案對學生學習的潛在影響後，將會批准該提案。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"approve of","explanation":"動詞片語，意為「贊同、滿意」。常用於表達對某人或某事物的認可。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many parents do not <approve> *of* their children spending too much time playing video games online.","translation":"許多家長不贊同他們的孩子花太多時間在線上玩電子遊戲。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"approve...for","explanation":"動詞片語，表示「為...核准」某事物。常在正式的申請或審批場合出現。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The committee will <approve> the scholarship application *for* deserving students who meet the academic criteria.","translation":"委員會將為符合學術標準的優秀學生核准獎學金申請。"}]}],"related_forms":["approves (v. pl.)","approved (v. past)","approving (v. pres. p.)","approval (n.)"]}

{"headword":"apron","explanation":"此字彙指「圍裙」，是廚房或進行特定活動（如實驗、繪畫）時的常用服飾配件。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"apron","explanation":"名詞，指圍在腰部前後、用以保護衣物的布或皮革，常在烹飪、手工藝或實驗時穿著。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The chef tied his clean white <apron> securely around his waist before starting to prepare the elaborate dinner.","translation":"廚師在開始準備精緻的晚餐前，將乾淨的白色圍裙牢牢繫在腰間。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"kitchen apron","explanation":"廚房圍裙。專指用於烹飪或廚房工作的圍裙。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"She wore a colorful floral <kitchen> <apron> to protect her clothes from splashes while baking cookies.","translation":"她穿著一件色彩繽紛的碎花廚房圍裙，以免烘烤餅乾時被噴濺弄髒衣服。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"armed","explanation":"此字彙主要考其作為形容詞「武裝的、攜帶武器的」，以及片語 armed with。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"armed","explanation":"形容詞，指「裝備武器的」或「攜帶武器的」。在描述安全或衝突相關情境時出現。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The museum hired several <armed> guards to ensure the safety of the valuable historical artifacts on display.","translation":"博物館僱用了幾名武裝警衛，以確保展出的珍貴歷史文物安全。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"armed forces","explanation":"武裝部隊，泛指國家的軍隊、海軍、空軍等。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The government announced new policies to support members of the <armed> <forces> and their families.","translation":"政府宣布了新的政策，以支持武裝部隊的成員及其家屬。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"armed with","explanation":"片語，指「攜帶著（武器）」或引申為「掌握著（資訊、知識、工具）」。後者為更常見的引申義。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The student entered the debate competition <armed> *with* thorough research and compelling arguments.","translation":"這位學生帶著充分的研究和有說服力的論點進入了辯論比賽。"}]}],"related_forms":["arm (v.)","arms (n. pl.)"]}

{"headword":"arrest","explanation":"此字彙常考「逮捕」的用法，作為名詞和動詞都可能出現。片語 arrest sb.'s attention 也是重點。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"arrest","explanation":"作為名詞，指「逮捕」或「（停止）」。常與介詞 in, under, on 搭配使用。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The police took the suspect into <arrest> after gathering sufficient evidence of his involvement in the crime.","translation":"警方在收集到他涉案的充分證據後，將嫌疑人逮捕歸案。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"arrest","explanation":"作為動詞，指「逮捕」某人。是GSAT中常見的司法或法律情境詞彙。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The authorities decided to <arrest> the individuals suspected of planning the public disturbance.","translation":"當局決定逮捕涉嫌策劃擾亂公共秩序的個人。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"arrest sb.'s attention","explanation":"片語，意為「引起某人的注意」。表示某事物或某人成功吸引了人們的目光或興趣。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The unusual advertisement immediately <arrested> the <attention> of passersby on the busy city street.","translation":"這則不尋常的廣告立刻引起了繁忙市街上路人的注意。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"under arrest","explanation":"片語，意為「被逮捕中」。描述某人被警方拘留的狀態。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The witness saw the police car take the suspect away while he was still <under> <arrest>.","translation":"目擊者看到警車將嫌疑人帶走，當時他仍處於被逮捕狀態。"}]}],"related_forms":["arrests (v. pl.)","arrested (v. past)"]}

{"headword":"ash","explanation":"這個字主要考「灰燼」的意思，特別是與火災或燃燒相關的語境。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"ash","explanation":"指燃燒後留下的粉末狀殘餘物，例如木材、香菸等燃燒後的灰。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After the devastating wildfire, thick <ash> covered the ground, making it difficult for the residents to return to their homes.","translation":"在毀滅性的野火之後，厚厚的灰燼覆蓋了地面，居民難以返回家園。"}]}],"related_forms":["ashes (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"aside","explanation":"這個副詞有「放在一邊」或「另外」的用法，常與動詞片語連用。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"aside","explanation":"指「在一旁」、「另外」。常用於 put aside (將...放在一旁) 或 set aside (擱置)。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The dedicated student decided to <aside> all social activities temporarily to focus on preparing for the upcoming college entrance examinations.","translation":"這位認真的學生決定暫時擱置所有社交活動，專注於準備即將到來的升學考試。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"aside from","explanation":"片語，意為「除了...之外（不包含）」或「不考慮」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"<Aside> from a slight fever, the patient showed no other symptoms, which puzzled the doctors during the initial diagnosis.","translation":"除了輕微發燒外，病患沒有表現出其他症狀，這讓醫生在初步診斷時感到困惑。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"assist","explanation":"這個動詞指「提供幫助」或「支援」，是比較正式的說法。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"assist","explanation":"提供幫助、支援。常與介詞 in 或 with 搭配使用，表示在某方面給予協助。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The experienced professor will <assist> the new researchers in designing their experimental procedures for the groundbreaking study.","translation":"經驗豐富的教授將協助新進研究人員設計他們在開創性研究中的實驗程序。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"assist sb. with sth.","explanation":"「協助某人處理某事」。強調在特定事務上的幫助。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The volunteers were organized to <assist> the elderly residents <with> their daily chores and provide companionship.","translation":"志工們被組織起來協助年長居民處理日常雜務並提供陪伴。"}]}],"related_forms":["assistance (n.)","assistant (n.)"]}

{"headword":"assistant","explanation":"這個名詞指「助手」或「助理」，是 assist 的相關詞。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"assistant","explanation":"指幫助他人工作的人，例如老師的助手 (teaching assistant) 或研究助理 (research assistant)。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The university is looking for a qualified <assistant> to help manage the laboratory equipment and supervise student experiments.","translation":"大學正在尋找一位合格的助理來協助管理實驗室設備並監督學生的實驗。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"teaching assistant","explanation":"「助教」。負責協助教授進行教學活動。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The graduate student successfully applied for the position of <teaching> <assistant>, which would provide him with valuable practical experience.","translation":"該研究生成功申請到助教職位，這將為他提供寶貴的實務經驗。"}]}],"related_forms":["assist (v.)","assistance (n.)"]}

{"headword":"assume","explanation":"這個動詞有「假設」、「假定」、「 Assume + to be」或「 Assume + that」等用法，且有「承擔」的意涵。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"assume","explanation":"動詞，意為「假設」、「假定」。常跟 that 子句連用，表示沒有實際證據下的推測。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"We cannot <assume> that all students fully understand the complex instructions without further clarification or examples being provided.","translation":"在沒有提供進一步說明或範例的情況下，我們不能假設所有學生都完全理解這些複雜的指示。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"assume responsibility","explanation":"「承擔責任」。指接受並處理某項任務或義務。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The team leader decided to <assume> full <responsibility> for the project's success or failure, motivating everyone to work harder.","translation":"團隊領導決定承擔項目成敗的全部責任，激勵大家更加努力。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"assume (sth. to be true)","explanation":"「假定（某事為真）」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"For the purpose of this scientific experiment, we <assume> the given data <to be> accurate and reliable without any prior verification.","translation":"為了這次科學實驗的目的，我們假定給定的數據是準確可靠的，無需事先驗證。"}]}],"related_forms":["assumption (n.)","assumed (adj.)"]}

{"headword":"athlete","explanation":"這個字指「運動員」。需注意其單複數及相關詞彙。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"athlete","explanation":"指在體育運動方面有技巧或力量的人，也指參加體育競賽的人。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The young <athlete> trained rigorously every day, hoping to qualify for the upcoming national track and field competition.","translation":"這位年輕的運動員每天都嚴格訓練，希望能獲得即將舉行的全國田徑賽的參賽資格。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"athletic","explanation":"形容詞，指「運動的」、「體格健美的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The school encourages students to participate in <athletic> activities, believing that physical fitness is as important as academic achievement.","translation":"學校鼓勵學生參加體育活動，相信體能與學業成就同等重要。"}]}],"related_forms":["athletes (pl.)","athletic"]}

{"headword":"attitude","explanation":"這個字表示「態度」或「看法」。在學術和社交場合都非常常見。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"attitude","explanation":"指一個人對某事物、某人或某情況的看法、想法或行為方式。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"A positive <attitude> towards learning is crucial for students to overcome challenges and achieve their academic goals effectively.","translation":"積極的學習態度對於學生克服挑戰並有效達成學業目標至關重要。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"attitude of...","explanation":"「attitude of...」表示「...的態度」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The company values employees who demonstrate a cooperative <attitude> *of* teamwork and mutual respect in their daily work.","translation":"公司重視那些在日常工作中展現合作態度、團隊精神和相互尊重的員工。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"attract","explanation":"這個字是動詞，意思是「吸引」。常用於描述人、事物或地點的吸引力。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"attract","explanation":"使人對某事物產生興趣，或使人想要靠近、參與。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The museum hopes to <attract> more visitors by showcasing its newly acquired collection of ancient artifacts from various civilizations.","translation":"該博物館希望透過展示其新近收購的、來自不同文明的古代文物藏品來吸引更多參觀者。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"attract attention","explanation":"「attract attention」是「引起注意」的意思。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The unusual brightly colored billboard was designed specifically to <attract> the attention of passersby on the busy street.","translation":"這個不同尋常的鮮豔廣告牌是特意設計來吸引繁忙街道上路人注意的。"}]}],"related_forms":["attracts","attracted","attracting","attraction","attractive"]}

{"headword":"attractive","explanation":"形容詞，指「有吸引力的」。用於形容人、物或地點。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"attractive","explanation":"使人產生興趣或好感的；具有吸引力的。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The university offers several scholarship programs to make its prestigious academic courses more <attractive> to talented students worldwide.","translation":"該大學提供多項獎學金計畫，以使其享有盛譽的學術課程對全球有才華的學生更具吸引力。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"be attractive to","explanation":"「be attractive to」意思是「對...有吸引力」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"This scenic coastal town is particularly <attractive> *to* tourists seeking a peaceful and relaxing vacation away from the city.","translation":"這個風景優美的海濱小鎮對尋求遠離城市、寧靜放鬆假期的遊客尤其有吸引力。"}]}],"related_forms":["attract","attracts","attracted","attracting","attraction"]}

{"headword":"audience","explanation":"這個字指「聽眾」、「觀眾」。常出現在與演講、表演或媒體相關的考題中。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"audience","explanation":"指觀看戲劇、電影、演出或聆聽演講、音樂會的人群。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The speaker adjusted his tone and pace to better engage the diverse <audience> gathered in the university auditorium for the lecture.","translation":"講者調整了他的語氣和節奏，以便更好地與聚集在大學禮堂聽講座的多元聽眾互動。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"for a... audience","explanation":"「for a... audience」表示「為...的聽眾/觀眾」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The documentary was carefully produced with young students *for* a general <audience>, making complex scientific concepts easy to understand.","translation":"這部紀錄片是為廣大觀眾精心製作的，讓複雜的科學概念易於理解，特別是針對年輕學生。"}]}],"related_forms":["audiences (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"automatic","explanation":"這個字主要考驗其「自動的」、「不自覺的」或「機械式的」用法，常與名詞搭配使用。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"automatic","explanation":"指「自動的」，不需人工操作。另外也可指「機械式的」或「不自覺的」反應。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The new coffee machine is fully <automatic>, making it convenient for busy students to prepare their morning beverage quickly.","translation":"這台新的咖啡機是全自動的，讓忙碌的學生能夠方便快速地準備他們的早晨飲品。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"automatically","explanation":"副詞，意為「自動地」、「機械地」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The library system <automatically> renews borrowed books if no one else has reserved them, saving students a trip to the circulation desk.","translation":"如果沒有其他人預約，圖書館系統會自動續借借閱的書籍，省去學生跑一趟流通櫃台的麻煩。"}]}],"related_forms":["automatically adv."]}

{"headword":"automobile","explanation":"這個字是「汽車」的正式說法，在 GSAT 中較常考其複合詞 'automobile industry' (汽車工業) 或簡稱 'auto'。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"automobile","explanation":"指「汽車」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The development of the <automobile> significantly transformed urban landscapes and influenced the growth of suburban communities worldwide.","translation":"汽車的發展顯著改變了城市景觀，並影響了全球郊區社區的成長。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"auto","explanation":"「automobile」的簡稱，指「汽車」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many university students rely on their <auto> to commute to campus, especially those living far from the main school buildings.","translation":"許多大學生依賴他們的汽車通勤到校園，特別是那些住在離校園主要建築很遠的學生。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"automobile industry","explanation":"「汽車工業」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The global <automobile industry> faces challenges from new technologies and shifting consumer preferences towards electric vehicles.","translation":"全球汽車工業面臨來自新技術和消費者對電動車偏好轉變的挑戰。"}]}],"related_forms":["autos (pl.)","automotive adj."]}

{"headword":"avenue","explanation":"這個字常考指「（寬闊的）大道」，也可用於比喻「途徑」或「方法」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"avenue","explanation":"指「（寬闊的）大道」，尤指兩旁有樹木的。也可指「解決問題的途徑」或「方法」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The main <avenue> leading to the city center is lined with historical buildings and charming cafes, attracting many tourists.","translation":"通往市中心的這條主要大道兩旁排列著歷史建築和迷人的咖啡館，吸引了許多遊客。"},{"marked_sentence":"The government is exploring every possible <avenue> to improve public transportation and reduce traffic congestion in the metropolitan area.","translation":"政府正在探索所有可能的途徑來改善公共交通並減少都會區的交通擁堵。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"awake","explanation":"這個字可作形容詞或動詞，意為「醒著的」或「喚醒」。注意其動詞過去式與過去分詞。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"awake","explanation":"形容詞，指「醒著的」。動詞，指「醒來」或「叫醒」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Even though it was past midnight, the student remained <awake>, diligently studying for the upcoming final examination at the library.","translation":"儘管已經過了午夜，這位學生仍然醒著，在圖書館勤奮地為即將到來的期末考試而學習。"},{"marked_sentence":"The loud noise from the construction site outside <awake> me from my deep sleep early in the morning, making it hard to go back to bed.","translation":"外面施工現場的巨大噪音在清晨把我從沉睡中喚醒，使我難以重新入睡。"}]}],"related_forms":["awoke (past tense)","awoken (past participle)","awaken v."]}

{"headword":"awaken","explanation":"這個字是動詞，意為「喚醒」、「叫醒」或「使意識到」。注意其動詞過去式與過去分詞。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"awaken","explanation":"動詞，指「喚醒」、「叫醒」或「使意識到」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The documentary aimed to <awaken> viewers' awareness of the critical environmental issues facing our planet today.","translation":"這部紀錄片旨在喚醒觀眾對當今我們星球面臨的嚴峻環境問題的意識。"},{"marked_sentence":"The smell of freshly baked bread usually <awaken> my appetite in the morning, even if I'm not particularly hungry.","translation":"剛出爐麵包的香味通常會在早上喚醒我的食慾，即使我並不特別餓。"}]}],"related_forms":["awakened (past tense)","awakened (past participle)","awake adj./v."]}

{"headword":"award","explanation":"這個字主要考「獎勵」、「獎項」的用法，動詞則表示「頒發」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"award","explanation":"指「獎品」、「獎勵」或「獎狀」。常與 specific award (具體的獎項) 連用。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The prestigious <award> ceremony celebrated outstanding achievements in various fields, recognizing excellence and dedication from professionals worldwide.","translation":"著名的頒獎典禮慶祝了各個領域的傑出成就，表彰了來自世界各地專業人士的卓越和奉獻。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"award a prize/medal/scholarship","explanation":"動詞片語，意為「頒發獎品／獎章／獎學金」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The university decided to <award> a full scholarship to the valedictorian for her exceptional academic performance throughout her high school years.","translation":"大學決定頒發全額獎學金給這位以其高中時期優異學業成績獲得的畢業生致詞代表。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"be awarded","explanation":"被動語態，表示「獲得獎項」或「被授予」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After years of dedicated research, Dr. Lee was honored to <be> <awarded> the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for his groundbreaking discoveries.","translation":"經過多年的專注研究，李博士很榮幸因其開創性的發現而獲得諾貝爾化學獎。"}]}],"related_forms":["awarded","awarding"]}

{"headword":"aware","explanation":"形容詞，表示「知道」、「意識到」。常與 of 搭配使用，形成 aware of sth.，強調對某事物的察覺。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"aware","explanation":"強調「意識到」或「了解」某情況。在口語和書面語中都很常見。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"It is crucial for students to be <aware> of the potential risks associated with cyberbullying and online privacy before using social media platforms.","translation":"學生在使用社群媒體平台前，至關重要是能意識到與網路霸凌和線上隱私相關的潛在風險。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"become aware of","explanation":"「開始意識到」某事。表示一個轉變或覺察的過程。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many people only <become> <aware> *of* the importance of environmental protection after experiencing severe natural disasters firsthand.","translation":"許多人在親身經歷嚴重自然災害後，才開始意識到環境保護的重要性。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"make someone aware of","explanation":"「使某人意識到」某事。常用於提醒或告知。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The teacher tried to <make> the students <aware> *of* the deadline for the project, reminding them to submit their work on time.","translation":"老師試圖讓學生意識到專案的截止日期，提醒他們準時繳交作業。"}]}],"related_forms":["awareness"]}

{"headword":"awful","explanation":"形容詞，表示「極其糟糕的」、「可怕的」。有時也用於加強語氣，表示「非常」，但這種用法較口語化，需注意。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"awful","explanation":"形容「令人不愉快的」、「糟糕的」或「可怕的」。例如 awful weather (惡劣天氣)。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The sudden change in weather brought <awful> conditions, making it difficult for drivers to navigate the roads safely due to the heavy rain.","translation":"天氣的突然變化帶來了惡劣的狀況，由於大雨，使得駕駛人難以安全地在道路上行駛。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"awful smell","explanation":"「難聞的氣味」。這個組合在描述不好的事物時很常見。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"There was an <awful> <smell> coming from the garbage bins near the school cafeteria, attracting flies and causing discomfort to students.","translation":"學校餐廳旁的垃圾桶散發出難聞的氣味，吸引了蒼蠅並讓學生感到不適。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"awfully sorry","explanation":"「非常抱歉」。口語中用來加強歉意。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"I am <awfully> sorry for being late to the important meeting; I promise it won't happen again in the future.","translation":"我對遲到重要的會議感到非常抱歉；我保證以後不會再發生了。"}]}],"related_forms":["awfully"]}

{"headword":"awkward","explanation":"形容詞，表示「笨拙的」、「尷尬的」或「棘手的」。常考其形容人或情境的用法。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"awkward","explanation":"形容「令人感到尷尬」或「不自在」的處境或人。例如 awkward silence (尷尬的沉默)。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"There was an <awkward> silence in the classroom after the student gave a surprisingly honest and personal answer to the teacher's question.","translation":"當該名學生對老師的問題給出一個出乎意料的誠實且個人化的回答後，教室裡出現了尷尬的沉默。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"awkward situation","explanation":"「棘手的局面」或「尷尬的狀況」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"He found himself in an <awkward> <situation> when he accidentally bumped into his ex-girlfriend while on a date with his current partner.","translation":"當他與現任伴侶約會時，不小心撞見前女友，讓他陷入一個尷尬的處境。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"awkward movement","explanation":"「笨拙的動作」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The new student's <awkward> <movement> on the basketball court suggested that he was still adjusting to the team's fast-paced playing style.","translation":"這位新學生在籃球場上笨拙的動作表明他仍在適應球隊快節奏的打法。"}]}],"related_forms":["awkwardly","awkwardness"]}

{"headword":"background","explanation":"名詞，指「背景」，涵蓋個人經歷、社會文化環境或圖片/影像的背景。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"background","explanation":"可指「個人經歷」、「出身背景」或「畫面、照片的背景」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The company values diverse employees, considering their educational qualifications and professional <background> when hiring new staff members.","translation":"公司重視多元化的員工，在聘用新員工時會考慮他們的教育資格和專業背景。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"background information","explanation":"「背景資料」或「背景資訊」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Before discussing the complex issue, the professor provided some essential <background> <information> to ensure all students understood the context.","translation":"在討論這個複雜的問題之前，教授提供了一些重要的背景資訊，以確保所有學生都理解其背景。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"on the background","explanation":"「在背景上」。常用於描述圖片或藝術作品。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The artist skillfully placed the main subject in the foreground, with a blurred landscape visible *on* the <background> of the painting.","translation":"藝術家巧妙地將主體放置在前景，畫布的背景上可見模糊的風景。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}bacon
column5: {"headword":"bacon","explanation":"培根是不可數名詞，指豬肉製成的煙燻肉片，常用於早餐。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bacon","explanation":"指「培根」，通常是不可數名詞。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"For breakfast, many students enjoy crispy <bacon> served with eggs and toast, a common meal in Western countries.","translation":"許多學生早餐喜歡吃酥脆的培根搭配雞蛋和吐司，這是西方國家常見的一餐。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

response: {"headword":"bacteria","explanation":"這個字是複數名詞，單數形為 bacterium，指「細菌」。在科學或健康議題中很常見。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bacteria","explanation":"指「細菌」，是複數名詞。其單數形為 bacterium。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Invisible <bacteria> can be found everywhere, some beneficial for digestion, while others cause serious infections in the human body.","translation":"看不見的細菌無處不在，有些對消化有益，而另一些則會導致人體嚴重感染。"}]}],"related_forms":["bacterium (sing.)"]}

{"headword":"badly","explanation":"副詞，意為「嚴重地」、「糟糕地」或「非常地」。常考其程度副詞的用法。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"badly","explanation":"副詞，表示「嚴重地」、「糟糕地」或「非常地」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After the car accident, the driver was <badly> injured and immediately rushed to the hospital for emergency treatment.","translation":"車禍後，司機受傷嚴重，立即被送往醫院進行緊急治療。"},{"marked_sentence":"The student performed <badly> *on* the math test because he had not studied enough for the difficult questions.","translation":"這名學生數學考試考得很差，因為他對那些難題沒有足夠的準備。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"want badly","explanation":"表示「非常想要」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"She <badly> *wants* to get into a top university, so she studies diligently every day to achieve her academic goals.","translation":"她非常想進入一所頂尖大學，所以她每天努力學習以實現她的學術目標。"}]}],"related_forms":["bad (adj.)","worse (comp.)","worst (sup.)"]}

{"headword":"baggage","explanation":"不可數名詞，指「行李」。在機場或旅行情境中常見。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"baggage","explanation":"指「行李」，是不可數名詞。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Passengers must check their <baggage> at the counter before proceeding to the security checkpoint for their international flight.","translation":"乘客必須在櫃檯託運行李，然後才能前往安全檢查站搭乘國際航班。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"luggage","explanation":"baggage 的同義詞，也是不可數名詞。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Please ensure your personal <luggage> is properly tagged and stored in the overhead compartment during the flight.","translation":"請確保您的個人行李已正確標籤並存放在飛行期間的頭頂置物櫃中。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"bait","explanation":"可作名詞或動詞。名詞指「誘餌」，動詞指「引誘」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bait (n.)","explanation":"名詞，指「誘餌」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The fisherman carefully put the worm *on* the hook as <bait>, hoping to catch a big fish in the river.","translation":"漁夫小心翼翼地將蟲子作為誘餌放在魚鉤上，希望能在河裡釣到一條大魚。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bait (v.)","explanation":"動詞，指「引誘」、「下誘餌」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The company tried to <bait> new customers *with* attractive discounts, but many were still hesitant to purchase their products.","translation":"該公司試圖用誘人的折扣來引誘新客戶，但許多人仍然猶豫是否購買他們的產品。"}]}],"related_forms":["baited (adj.)","baiting (v.ing)"]}

{"headword":"bamboo","explanation":"竹子是亞洲文化中常見的植物，在閱讀測驗中可能作為文化背景或生態主題出現。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bamboo","explanation":"指「竹子」或「竹材」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The giant panda's diet consists almost entirely of <bamboo>, highlighting its unique adaptation to a specific food source.","translation":"大熊貓的飲食幾乎完全由竹子組成，突顯了牠對特定食物來源的獨特適應性。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"bang","explanation":"這個字可以作名詞、動詞或副詞，表示「砰」的聲音或動作，在聽力或閱讀中可能出現。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bang","explanation":"作名詞時，指「巨響」、「砰的一聲」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Suddenly, there was a loud <bang> *at* the door, startling everyone in the quiet library.","translation":"突然，門口傳來一聲巨響，嚇壞了圖書館裡所有安靜的人。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bang","explanation":"作動詞時，指「猛擊」、「砰地關上」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Frustrated by the argument, he <bang>ed his fist *on* the table, causing a sudden silence in the room.","translation":"因為爭吵而沮喪，他猛地用拳頭敲桌子，導致房間裡突然一片寂靜。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bang","explanation":"作副詞時，指「砰地一聲」。常用於口語。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The car went <bang> *into* the wall, causing significant damage and attracting immediate attention from passersby.","translation":"那輛車砰地一聲撞上了牆，造成了嚴重的損壞，立刻引起了路人的注意。"}]}],"related_forms":["banged (v. past)","banging (v. ing)"]}

{"headword":"banker","explanation":"指「銀行家」，是金融領域的專業人士，可能在商業或經濟相關的文章中出現。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"banker","explanation":"指「銀行家」，從事銀行業的高級職員。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The experienced <banker> offered valuable financial advice to the startup company, helping them secure necessary investments.","translation":"這位經驗豐富的銀行家為這家新創公司提供了寶貴的財務建議，幫助他們獲得了必要的投資。"}]}],"related_forms":["bank (n.)","banking (n.)"]}

{"headword":"bare","explanation":"這個字作形容詞時指「赤裸的」、「光禿的」，作動詞時指「揭露」。注意其多重詞性。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bare","explanation":"作形容詞時，指「赤裸的」、「光禿的」、「空的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After the fire, the trees stood <bare> *of* leaves, a stark reminder of the devastating impact on the forest.","translation":"火災過後，樹木光禿禿地沒有葉子，鮮明地提醒著森林所遭受的毀滅性影響。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bare","explanation":"作動詞時，指「揭露」、「露出」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The investigative report <bare>d the truth *about* the company's unethical practices, leading to public outrage and legal action.","translation":"調查報告揭露了該公司不道德行為的真相，引發了公眾的憤怒和法律訴訟。"}]}],"related_forms":["barely (adv.)","bared (v. past)","baring (v. ing)"]}

{"headword":"barely","explanation":"作副詞，意為「幾乎不」、「勉強地」，常帶有否定意味，是閱讀測驗中判斷語氣的重要線索。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"barely","explanation":"指「幾乎不」、「勉強地」，表示程度很低或困難地完成。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The student had <barely> enough time *to* finish the challenging exam, submitting it just as the bell rang.","translation":"這名學生勉強有足夠的時間完成這項具挑戰性的考試，在鈴響時剛好交卷。"}]}],"related_forms":["bare (adj./v.)"]}

{"headword":"barn","explanation":"這個字指「穀倉」或「大倉庫」，在閱讀測驗中常與農業相關主題出現。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"barn","explanation":"指農場中儲存穀物、乾草或飼養牲畜的建築物。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The farmer stored all the freshly harvested corn in the large <barn> to protect it from the rain and pests.","translation":"農夫將所有剛收成的玉米儲存在大穀倉裡，以保護它免受雨水和害蟲的侵害。"}]}],"related_forms":["barns (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"barrel","explanation":"這個字指「桶子」，在測驗中可能出現在描述儲存液體或貨物的語境中。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"barrel","explanation":"指圓柱形、通常由木材製成的容器，用於儲存液體（如酒）或散裝貨物。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The old winery kept its finest wines aging in large oak <barrels> in a cool, dark cellar for many years.","translation":"這家老酒莊將其最上等的葡萄酒放在涼爽黑暗的地窖裡的大橡木桶中陳釀多年。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"barrel of laughs","explanation":"口語用法，意為「非常有趣的人或事」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Despite the difficult exam, our study group was a real <barrel> *of* laughs, making the preparation much more enjoyable.","translation":"儘管考試很難，但我們的讀書小組真是個開心果，讓準備過程愉快許多。"}]}],"related_forms":["barrels (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"basement","explanation":"這個字指「地下室」，常在描述建築物結構或儲藏空間的語境中出現。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"basement","explanation":"指建築物地面以下的樓層，通常用於儲藏或作為額外空間。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"During the typhoon, the residents moved their valuable belongings to the dry <basement> to protect them from potential flooding.","translation":"颱風期間，居民將貴重物品搬到乾燥的地下室，以保護它們免受潛在的洪水侵害。"}]}],"related_forms":["basements (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"bay","explanation":"這個字有多重意思，常見的有「海灣」和「停車格/隔間」。在閱讀測驗中需根據上下文判斷。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bay","explanation":"指「海灣」，是陸地向內凹陷的水域。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The small fishing village was nestled in a beautiful, sheltered <bay>, offering a safe harbor for boats during storms.","translation":"這個小漁村坐落在一個美麗、受保護的海灣中，為船隻在暴風雨期間提供了安全的港口。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bay","explanation":"指「停車格」或「隔間」，例如 loading bay (裝卸區)。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The delivery truck carefully backed into the loading <bay> at the supermarket to unload the fresh produce for the day.","translation":"送貨卡車小心翼翼地倒車進入超市的裝卸區，卸下當天的新鮮農產品。"}]}],"related_forms":["bays (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"bead","explanation":"這個字指「珠子」或「水珠」，在描述裝飾品或液體凝結時可能出現。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bead","explanation":"指「珠子」，常用於製作項鍊、手鍊等飾品。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"She carefully strung each colorful glass <bead> onto the thread, creating a unique and beautiful necklace for her friend.","translation":"她小心翼翼地將每一顆彩色玻璃珠串到線上，為她的朋友製作了一條獨特而美麗的項鍊。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bead of sweat","explanation":"指「汗珠」，常用於描述出汗的狀態。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After running the marathon, a <bead> *of* sweat trickled down his forehead, showing the intensity of his effort.","translation":"跑完馬拉松後，一滴汗珠從他的額頭上滴落，顯示出他付出的努力。"}]}],"related_forms":["beads (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"beam","explanation":"這個字可作名詞和動詞。名詞常指「光線」或「橫樑」，動詞則指「發光」或「微笑」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"beam","explanation":"作名詞時，指「光線」或「光束」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"A powerful <beam> of light from the lighthouse guided the ships safely through the stormy night.","translation":"燈塔射出的一道強光引導船隻安全度過暴風雨的夜晚。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"beam","explanation":"作名詞時，也可指建築物中的「橫樑」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The old house's wooden <beam>s needed repair to ensure the structural integrity of the entire building.","translation":"這棟老房子的木製橫樑需要修理，以確保整棟建築的結構完整性。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"beam","explanation":"作動詞時，指「眉開眼笑」或「發出光芒」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The student <beam>ed with pride after receiving the highest score on the challenging final exam.","translation":"這位學生在具挑戰性的期末考中獲得最高分後，驕傲地眉開眼笑。"}]}],"related_forms":["beaming","beamed"]}

{"headword":"beast","explanation":"這個字作名詞，指「野獸」或「兇猛的動物」。在文學或比喻中也常用。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"beast","explanation":"指「野獸」、「兇猛的動物」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The ancient legend tells of a fearsome <beast> that roamed the dark forests, terrifying local villagers.","translation":"古老的傳說講述了一隻在黑暗森林中漫遊、讓當地村民感到恐懼的可怕野獸。"}]}],"related_forms":["beasts","beastly"]}

{"headword":"beetle","explanation":"這個字作名詞，指「甲蟲」。常出現在生物學或自然科學的語境中。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"beetle","explanation":"指「甲蟲」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The scientist carefully observed the colorful <beetle> as it crawled across the leaf, studying its unique patterns.","translation":"科學家仔細觀察這隻色彩斑斕的甲蟲在葉子上爬行，研究其獨特的圖案。"}]}],"related_forms":["beetles"]}

{"headword":"beneath","explanation":"這個字作介係詞，意為「在...下方」或「在...之下」。與 under 相似，但有時帶有更正式或文學的語氣。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"beneath","explanation":"作介係詞，意為「在...下方」、「在...之下」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The ancient city's ruins were discovered hidden *beneath* layers of soil and vegetation after centuries.","translation":"經過數百年，這座古城的遺跡被發現隱藏在層層土壤和植被之下。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"benefit","explanation":"這個字可作名詞和動詞。名詞指「好處」、「利益」，動詞指「有益於」或「受益」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"benefit","explanation":"作名詞時，指「好處」、「利益」。常與介係詞 *of* 或 *from* 連用。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Regular exercise offers numerous health <benefit>s, including improved mood and increased energy levels.","translation":"規律運動提供許多健康益處，包括改善情緒和提升能量水平。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"benefit","explanation":"作動詞時，意為「有益於」或「受益」。常與介係詞 *from* 連用。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Students will greatly <benefit> *from* participating in extracurricular activities, developing social skills.","translation":"學生將從參與課外活動中大大受益，培養社交技能。"}]}],"related_forms":["beneficial","benefited","benefiting"]}

{"headword":"berry","explanation":"這個字指「漿果」，常在描述食物或自然環境的語境中出現。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"berry","explanation":"指「漿果」，如草莓、藍莓等。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The hikers found a bush full of wild <berry> in the forest, which they identified as edible after checking their guide.","translation":"登山客在森林裡發現了一叢長滿野漿果的灌木，他們在查閱指南後確認這些漿果可以食用。"}]}],"related_forms":["berries (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"besides","explanation":"這個字可作副詞或介係詞，意思分別是「此外」和「除了...之外」。注意與 beside (在...旁邊) 的區別。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"besides (adv.)","explanation":"作副詞，意為「此外」、「而且」。通常用於句首或句中，補充說明。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The new school cafeteria offers healthy meals; <besides>, it provides a comfortable space for students to socialize during lunch breaks.","translation":"新的學校餐廳提供健康的餐點；此外，它也為學生在午餐時間提供了一個舒適的社交空間。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"besides (prep.)","explanation":"作介係詞，意為「除了...之外（還包括）」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"<Besides> English and Math, students in this program are required to take advanced courses *in* science and history to broaden their knowledge.","translation":"除了英文和數學之外，這個課程的學生還需要修習進階的科學和歷史課程以拓展他們的知識。"}]}],"related_forms":["beside (prep.)"]}

{"headword":"bet","explanation":"這個字可作動詞或名詞，指「打賭」。動詞三態同形，是常考的重點。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bet (v.)","explanation":"動詞，意為「打賭」。三態為 bet-bet-bet。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"I <bet> you that our school's basketball team will win the championship this year, given their excellent performance in recent games.","translation":"我跟你打賭我們學校的籃球隊今年會贏得冠軍，考慮到他們在最近比賽中的出色表現。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bet (n.)","explanation":"名詞，意為「賭注」、「打賭」。常用於 make a bet (打賭)。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The students made a friendly <bet> *on* who would get the highest score *on* the upcoming English vocabulary test.","translation":"學生們就誰會在即將到來的英文單字測驗中獲得最高分打了一個友誼賽的賭。"}]}],"related_forms":["betting (n.)"]}

{"headword":"bind","explanation":"這個字作動詞，意為「綑綁」、「約束」。其過去式和過去分詞為 bound，是常考的不規則動詞。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bind","explanation":"動詞，意為「綑綁」、「約束」。三態為 bind-bound-bound。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The ancient scroll was carefully preserved, its delicate pages <bound> together *with* a silk ribbon to prevent damage.","translation":"這份古老卷軸被小心翼翼地保存著，其脆弱的書頁用絲帶綑綁在一起以防止損壞。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"be bound to","explanation":"片語，意為「必定會...」、「注定會...」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Given rising global temperatures, scientists warn that extreme weather events are <bound> *to* become more frequent *in* the coming years.","translation":"鑑於全球氣溫不斷升高，科學家警告說，極端天氣事件在未來幾年內必定會變得更加頻繁。"}]}],"related_forms":["bound (adj.)","binding (n.)"]}

{"headword":"bitter","explanation":"這個字作形容詞，意為「苦的」、「痛苦的」、「嚴酷的」。在閱讀測驗中常出現其引申義。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bitter","explanation":"形容詞，指味道「苦的」，或情感上「痛苦的」、「怨恨的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After the unexpected defeat, the team felt a <bitter> disappointment, but they vowed to train harder for the next competition.","translation":"在出乎意料的失敗後，隊伍感到一陣苦澀的失望，但他們誓言要為下一場比賽更努力地訓練。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bitter cold","explanation":"形容詞片語，意為「嚴寒的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Despite the <bitter> cold, the determined students still attended the early morning study group to prepare for their final exams.","translation":"儘管天氣嚴寒，這些堅定的學生仍然參加了清晨的讀書會，為期末考做準備。"}]}],"related_forms":["bitterly (adv.)","bitterness (n.)"]}

{"headword":"bleed","explanation":"這個字主要指「流血」，常用於描述受傷或醫療情境。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bleed","explanation":"作動詞，意為「流血」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After falling off his bike, the boy's knee started to <bleed> heavily, requiring immediate medical attention at the clinic.","translation":"從自行車上摔下來後，男孩的膝蓋開始大量流血，需要立即到診所就醫。"}]}],"related_forms":["bled (past tense, past participle)"]}

{"headword":"bless","explanation":"這個字主要指「祝福」或「保佑」，常用於宗教或表達良好祝願的語境。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bless","explanation":"作動詞，意為「祝福；保佑」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The priest will <bless> the newlyweds during the ceremony, wishing them a lifetime of happiness and prosperity together.","translation":"牧師將在典禮中祝福這對新婚夫婦，祝願他們一生幸福美滿。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"be blessed with","explanation":"片語，意為「有幸擁有...；蒙受...恩惠」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"She is <blessed> *with* a natural talent for music, allowing her to play various instruments beautifully since childhood.","translation":"她天生擁有音樂天賦，從小就能優美地演奏各種樂器。"}]}],"related_forms":["blessed (past tense, past participle)","blessing (n.)"]}

{"headword":"bloody","explanation":"這個字作形容詞，主要指「血腥的」或「流血的」，有時也作口語的加強語氣詞。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bloody","explanation":"作形容詞，意為「血腥的；流血的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The detective found a <bloody> knife at the crime scene, which was crucial evidence for solving the mysterious case.","translation":"偵探在犯罪現場發現了一把帶血的刀，這是偵破這個神秘案件的關鍵證據。"}]}],"related_forms":["blood (n.)"]}

{"headword":"blouse","explanation":"這個字作名詞，指「女式襯衫」，是服裝類詞彙。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"blouse","explanation":"作名詞，意為「女式襯衫」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"She chose a white silk <blouse> and a black skirt for her job interview, hoping to make a good impression.","translation":"她選擇了一件白色絲質襯衫和黑色裙子參加面試，希望能給人留下好印象。"}]}],"related_forms":["blouses (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"bold","explanation":"這個字作形容詞，主要指「大膽的；勇敢的」，也可用於描述字體。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bold","explanation":"作形容詞，意為「大膽的；勇敢的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The student made a <bold> decision to pursue an unconventional major, despite her parents' initial concerns about her career path.","translation":"這位學生做了一個大膽的決定，選擇了一個非傳統的專業，儘管她的父母最初對她的職業道路有所擔憂。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bold","explanation":"作形容詞，意為「粗體的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Please highlight the key terms in <bold> font so that readers can easily identify the most important information in the report.","translation":"請將關鍵術語用粗體字標示出來，以便讀者能輕鬆辨識報告中最重要的資訊。"}]}],"related_forms":["boldly (adv.)","boldness (n.)"]}

{"headword":"bomb","explanation":"這個字可作名詞「炸彈」或動詞「轟炸」。動詞也常指「考試不及格」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bomb","explanation":"指「炸彈」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The police found an unexploded <bomb> near the construction site, leading to an immediate evacuation of the area.","translation":"警方在建築工地附近發現一枚未爆彈，導致該區域立即疏散。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bomb","explanation":"指「轟炸」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"During the war, enemy planes <bomb>ed the city, causing extensive damage to historical buildings and infrastructure.","translation":"戰爭期間，敵機轟炸了這座城市，對歷史建築和基礎設施造成了廣泛的破壞。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bomb","explanation":"動詞，口語上指「考試不及格」或「表現很差」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Despite studying hard for weeks, John was worried he might <bomb> the final physics exam.","translation":"儘管努力學習了數週，約翰還是擔心他可能會物理期末考不及格。"}]}],"related_forms":["bombs (pl.)","bombed (past tense)","bombing (present participle/gerund)"]}

{"headword":"bookcase","explanation":"這個字是複合名詞，指「書櫃」。注意其單複數變化。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bookcase","explanation":"指「書櫃」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"My new <bookcase> is filled with various novels and textbooks, making my study room look much tidier.","translation":"我的新書櫃裡擺滿了各式各樣的小說和教科書，讓我的書房看起來整潔多了。"}]}],"related_forms":["bookcases (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"boot","explanation":"這個字可作名詞「靴子」或動詞「啟動（電腦）」、「解僱」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"boot","explanation":"指「靴子」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"She wore a pair of sturdy hiking <boot>s for the mountain trail, ensuring her feet stayed dry and protected.","translation":"她穿了一雙堅固的登山靴去走山路，確保她的雙腳保持乾燥和受到保護。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"boot","explanation":"動詞，指「啟動（電腦）」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After installing the new software, I had to <boot> the computer again to apply all the changes.","translation":"安裝新軟體後，我必須重新啟動電腦才能應用所有更改。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"boot out","explanation":"動詞片語，指「解僱」或「驅逐」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The company decided to <boot> *out* several employees due to severe budget cuts and restructuring.","translation":"由於嚴重的預算削減和重組，公司決定解僱幾名員工。"}]}],"related_forms":["boots (pl.)","booted (past tense)","booting (present participle/gerund)"]}

{"headword":"bore","explanation":"這個字可作動詞「使厭煩」或名詞「令人厭煩的人事物」。注意其衍生形容詞 boring 和 bored。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bore","explanation":"動詞，指「使厭煩」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The professor's long and monotonous lecture began to <bore> the students, who started to check their phones.","translation":"教授冗長單調的演講開始讓學生感到厭煩，他們開始查看手機。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bore","explanation":"名詞，指「令人厭煩的人事物」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"That history documentary was such a <bore>; I fell asleep halfway through it despite my interest in the subject.","translation":"那部歷史紀錄片真是無聊透頂；儘管我對這個主題很感興趣，但我還是看到一半就睡著了。"}]}],"related_forms":["bores (3rd person singular)","bored (past tense/past participle/adj.)","boring (present participle/adj.)","boredom (n.)"]}

{"headword":"bowling","explanation":"這個字是名詞，指「保齡球運動」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bowling","explanation":"指「保齡球運動」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Our class decided to go <bowling> for our social event, and everyone had a great time trying to get strikes.","translation":"我們班決定去打保齡球作為社交活動，每個人都玩得很開心，努力想打出全倒。"}]}],"related_forms":["bowler (n.)"]}

{"headword":"brake","explanation":"這個字常考名詞「煞車」或動詞「煞車」。注意動詞的用法及片語。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"brake","explanation":"指「煞車裝置」。常用複數 brakes。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The car's <brakes> failed suddenly, causing the driver to lose control and narrowly avoid a collision on the busy highway.","translation":"汽車的煞車突然失靈，導致駕駛失控，險些在繁忙的高速公路上發生碰撞。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"brake","explanation":"指「煞車」的動作。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The cyclist had to <brake> hard to avoid hitting a pedestrian who suddenly stepped into the bike lane without looking.","translation":"騎自行車的人不得不緊急煞車，以避免撞到突然沒看路就走進自行車道上的行人。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"put the brakes on","explanation":"片語，意為「阻止某事發生」或「減緩某事」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The school board decided to *put* the <brakes> *on* the new curriculum proposal until further research could be conducted.","translation":"校董會決定暫緩新的課程提案，直到可以進行進一步的研究。"}]}],"related_forms":["brakes (pl. n.)","braking (v.ing)"]}

{"headword":"brass","explanation":"這個字常考名詞「黃銅」或形容詞「黃銅製的」。注意其在樂器或態度上的延伸意義。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"brass","explanation":"指「黃銅」這種金屬。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Many antique musical instruments, especially trumpets and trombones, are often made *of* <brass> due to its resonant qualities.","translation":"許多古董樂器，尤其是小號和長號，通常由黃銅製成，因為它具有共鳴的特性。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"brass","explanation":"指「銅管樂器」或「銅管樂隊」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The school orchestra's <brass> section played a powerful fanfare, filling the auditorium with rich, vibrant sounds during the concert.","translation":"學校管弦樂團的銅管樂器組演奏了一段氣勢磅礴的號角曲，在音樂會期間讓禮堂充滿了豐富而充滿活力的聲音。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"brass","explanation":"指「黃銅製的」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The old door had a beautiful <brass> knocker shaped like a lion's head, adding a touch of elegance to the entrance.","translation":"那扇舊門上裝有一個漂亮的黃銅門環，形狀像獅子頭，為入口增添了一絲優雅。"}]}],"related_forms":["brassy (adj.)"]}

{"headword":"bravery","explanation":"這個字是名詞，指「勇敢」。常與動詞 show, demonstrate, display 搭配。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bravery","explanation":"指「勇敢、勇氣」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The firefighter's <bravery> *in* rescuing the trapped family *from* the burning building was widely praised by the community.","translation":"消防員從燃燒的建築物中救出受困家庭的英勇行為受到社區的廣泛讚揚。"}]}],"related_forms":["brave (adj.)","bravely (adv.)"]}

{"headword":"breast","explanation":"這個字是名詞，指「胸部」或「乳房」。在醫學或動物學中常見。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"breast","explanation":"指「胸部」或「乳房」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The doctor advised regular self-examinations *of* the <breast> for early detection of any potential health issues.","translation":"醫生建議定期進行乳房自我檢查，以便及早發現任何潛在的健康問題。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"breast","explanation":"指禽類的「胸肉」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"For a healthier meal, many people choose chicken <breast> over other cuts because it is lean and high *in* protein.","translation":"為了更健康的飲食，許多人選擇雞胸肉而不是其他部位，因為它脂肪少且蛋白質含量高。"}]}],"related_forms":["breasts (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"breath","explanation":"這個字是名詞，指「呼吸」或「一口氣」。常考動詞片語。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"breath","explanation":"指「呼吸」或「一口氣」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After running the marathon, the athlete was out *of* <breath>, but proud *of* completing the challenging race.","translation":"跑完馬拉松後，這位運動員氣喘吁吁，但為完成這項艱鉅的比賽感到自豪。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"take a deep breath","explanation":"片語，意為「深呼吸」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Before giving her presentation, she *took* a deep <breath> to calm her nerves and focus on her speech.","translation":"在做簡報之前，她深呼吸以平復緊張情緒並專注於演講。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"hold one's breath","explanation":"片語，意為「屏住呼吸」或「懸著一顆心」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The audience *held* their <breath> as the magician performed his most dangerous trick, wondering if he would succeed.","translation":"魔術師表演他最危險的把戲時，觀眾屏住呼吸，想知道他是否會成功。"}]}],"related_forms":["breathe (v.)","breathless (adj.)"]}

{"headword":"breathe","explanation":"動詞，指「呼吸」。常與介係詞 in 或 out 搭配，表示吸氣或吐氣。注意與名詞 breath 的區別。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"breathe","explanation":"指「呼吸」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"After a long run, the athlete paused to <breathe> deeply, trying to regain his composure and energy.","translation":"長跑之後，運動員停下來深呼吸，試圖恢復鎮定和體力。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"breathe in/out","explanation":"動詞片語，指「吸氣」或「吐氣」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"During meditation, it's important to <breathe> *in* slowly through your nose and *out* through your mouth to relax.","translation":"冥想時，重要的是要透過鼻子緩慢吸氣，再透過嘴巴吐氣，以達到放鬆。"}]}],"related_forms":["breath (n.)","breathing (n.)"]}

{"headword":"breeze","explanation":"可作名詞或動詞。名詞指「微風」，動詞指「輕鬆地移動」。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"breeze (n.)","explanation":"名詞，指「微風」。常在閱讀測驗中描述天氣或環境。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"A gentle <breeze> rustled through the trees, providing a refreshing coolness on the warm summer afternoon.","translation":"一陣微風吹過樹林，在溫暖的夏日午後帶來了清爽的涼意。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"breeze (v.)","explanation":"動詞，指「輕鬆地移動或通過」。常與介係詞 through 搭配。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Despite the difficult questions, the top student managed to <breeze> *through* the challenging math exam with ease.","translation":"儘管問題很難，這位頂尖學生還是輕鬆地通過了這場具有挑戰性的數學考試。"}]}],"related_forms":["breezy (adj.)"]}

{"headword":"brick","explanation":"名詞，指「磚塊」。常用於描述建築材料或比喻堅固的事物。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"brick","explanation":"指「磚塊」。在閱讀測驗中常出現 brick house (磚房) 或 brick wall (磚牆)。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The old school building, constructed entirely of red <brick>, has stood for over a century, resisting countless storms.","translation":"這座完全由紅磚建造的老校舍，已經屹立了一個多世紀，抵擋了無數次風暴。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"hit a brick wall","explanation":"片語，比喻「遇到瓶頸或困難」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The research team *hit* a <brick> *wall* when they couldn't find any more data to support their hypothesis.","translation":"當研究團隊找不到更多數據來支持他們的假設時，他們遇到了瓶頸。"}]}],"related_forms":["bricks (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"bride","explanation":"名詞，指「新娘」。常用於描述婚禮相關情境。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bride","explanation":"指「新娘」。在閱讀測驗中常與 groom (新郎) 一起出現。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The <bride> looked stunning in her white gown as she walked down the aisle towards her eager groom.","translation":"新娘穿著白色禮服走下紅毯，走向她熱切的新郎，看起來美極了。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bride-to-be","explanation":"指「準新娘」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"My sister, a <bride>-*to*-*be*, is busy planning every detail of her upcoming wedding, from the venue to the flowers.","translation":"我的姊姊，一位準新娘，正忙著規劃她即將到來的婚禮的每一個細節，從場地到鮮花。"}]}],"related_forms":["bridal (adj.)","bridesmaid (n.)"]}

{"headword":"broadcast","explanation":"可作名詞或動詞。名詞指「廣播節目」，動詞指「廣播、播送」。動詞三態同形。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"broadcast (n.)","explanation":"名詞，指「廣播節目」或「播送」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The live <broadcast> of the national science competition attracted millions of viewers across the country.","translation":"全國科學競賽的現場直播吸引了全國數百萬觀眾。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"broadcast (v.)","explanation":"動詞，指「廣播、播送」。三態同形 (broadcast, broadcast, broadcast)。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The university decided to <broadcast> the graduation ceremony online so that international students' families could watch it live.","translation":"大學決定將畢業典禮在線上直播，以便國際學生的家人可以即時觀看。"}]}],"related_forms":["broadcaster (n.)","broadcasting (n.)"]}

{"headword":"brunch","explanation":"早午餐，是 breakfast 和 lunch 的結合，常考其概念。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"brunch","explanation":"指「早午餐」，通常在週末或假日享用。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"On Sundays, many families enjoy a leisurely <brunch> together, combining breakfast and lunch into one meal.","translation":"在星期天，許多家庭喜歡一起享用悠閒的早午餐，將早餐和午餐合併成一餐。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"bubble","explanation":"可作名詞或動詞，名詞指「氣泡」，動詞指「冒泡」。常考其物理現象或引申義。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bubble","explanation":"指「氣泡」。在科學或日常生活中常見。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The children laughed as they watched the soap <bubble> float into the sky, reflecting the sunlight.","translation":"孩子們看著肥皂泡泡飄向天空，反射著陽光，開心地笑了。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"bubble","explanation":"指「冒泡」或「沸騰」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"When the water started to <bubble> in the pot, she knew it was hot enough to add the pasta for dinner.","translation":"當水在鍋裡開始冒泡時，她知道水夠熱可以加入義大利麵準備晚餐了。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"burst one's bubble","explanation":"片語，意為「打破某人的幻想/美夢」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"I hate to *burst* your <bubble>, but the concert tickets are already sold out, so we can't go tonight.","translation":"我不想打破你的美夢，但演唱會門票已經售罄，所以我們今晚去不了了。"}]}],"related_forms":["bubbles (pl.)","bubbling (adj.)"]}

{"headword":"bucket","explanation":"名詞，指「水桶」。常考其字面意義或搭配。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bucket","explanation":"指「水桶」，用於盛水或其他液體。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"He filled the <bucket> *with* water from the tap and carried it to the garden to water the thirsty plants.","translation":"他用水龍頭將水桶裝滿水，然後把它提到花園去澆灌那些口渴的植物。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"a bucket list","explanation":"片語，指「人生願望清單」，即在死前想完成的事情。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Traveling around the world has always been *on* her <bucket> list, and she plans to start next year after graduation.","translation":"環遊世界一直是她的人生願望清單上的項目，她計劃畢業後明年開始。"}]}],"related_forms":["buckets (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"bud","explanation":"可作名詞或動詞，名詞指「花苞」、「芽」，動詞指「發芽」。常考其自然現象或引申義。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bud","explanation":"指「花苞」或「芽」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"In early spring, tiny green <bud>s began to appear *on* the branches, signaling the arrival of warmer weather.","translation":"早春時節，樹枝上開始出現小小的綠芽，預示著溫暖天氣的到來。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"nip something in the bud","explanation":"片語，意為「防患於未然」、「將某事扼殺在萌芽狀態」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The teacher decided to *nip* the bullying problem *in* the <bud> by addressing it immediately with all the students.","translation":"老師決定立即與所有學生討論霸凌問題，將其扼殺在萌芽狀態。"}]}],"related_forms":["buds (pl.)","budding (adj.)"]}

{"headword":"budget","explanation":"可作名詞或動詞，名詞指「預算」，動詞指「編列預算」。在經濟或個人理財方面常考。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"budget","explanation":"指「預算」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The school committee approved a generous <budget> *for* the new science lab, allowing for advanced equipment purchases.","translation":"學校委員會批准了一筆用於新科學實驗室的豐厚預算，允許購買先進設備。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"budget","explanation":"指「編列預算」或「節約開支」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"To afford the trip to Japan, she had to carefully <budget> her expenses every month, saving a little from each paycheck.","translation":"為了負擔日本之旅，她每個月都必須仔細編列開支，從每份薪水中省下一點錢。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"on a budget","explanation":"片語，意為「預算有限」、「節省開支」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Even *on* a tight <budget>, it's possible to eat healthy meals if you plan your grocery shopping wisely.","translation":"即使預算有限，如果你明智地規劃採購，仍然可以吃到健康的餐點。"}]}],"related_forms":["budgets (pl.)","budgeted (adj.)"]}

{"headword":"buffalo","explanation":"指「水牛」或「野牛」。在閱讀測驗中可能與動物生態或地理環境相關。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"buffalo","explanation":"指「水牛」或「野牛」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The American <buffalo>, also known as the bison, once roamed vast grasslands, playing a crucial role *in* the ecosystem.","translation":"美國野牛，也被稱為北美野牛，曾漫遊於廣闊的草原，在生態系統中扮演著關鍵角色。"}]}],"related_forms":["buffaloes (pl.)"]}

{"headword":"buffet","explanation":"這個字主要指「自助餐」，在生活中很常見，也可能在閱讀測驗中出現。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"buffet","explanation":"指「自助餐」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"The hotel offers a delicious breakfast <buffet> *with* a wide selection of dishes, attracting many guests every morning.","translation":"這家飯店提供美味的自助早餐，菜色豐富，每天早上都吸引許多客人。"}]}],"related_forms":[]}

{"headword":"bulb","explanation":"主要指「燈泡」或植物的「球莖」。在科技或生物學相關的文章中可能出現。","entries":[{"word_or_collocation":"bulb","explanation":"指「燈泡」。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"We need to replace the burnt-out light <bulb> *in* the living room to ensure proper illumination for reading.","translation":"我們需要更換客廳裡燒壞的燈泡，以確保閱讀時有足夠的照明。"}]},{"word_or_collocation":"light bulb","explanation":"「燈泡」的常見說法。","sentences":[{"marked_sentence":"Thomas Edison is widely credited *with* inventing the practical electric <light bulb>, revolutionizing how people live and work.","translation":"湯瑪斯·愛迪生因發明實用的電燈泡而廣受讚譽，徹底改變了人們的生活和工作方式。"}]}],"related_forms":["bulbs (pl.)"]}
